title: Docker Compose on a single VPS for production
description: Production runs as a Docker Compose stack on one dedicated VPS (4 vCPU / 16GB / 200GB SSD). Backups to an S3-compatible bucket nightly. No orchestrator until we have a second machine in the mix.
rationale: We have one product, one team, and traffic that fits comfortably on one machine. A managed Kubernetes cluster would multiply our ops surface area without buying us anything we need this quarter.
trade_offs: Single-host outage = full outage; downtime budget assumes this
Vertical scaling only until we move to multi-host
alternatives_considered: Kubernetes (managed) — rejected: operational overhead too high for current scale
Serverless / Lambda — rejected: workload includes long-running websockets and background workers
Heroku / Render — rejected: cost at our load profile, vendor lock-in
restore_step: If the VPS dies: provision a fresh box from the same provider, restore the latest S3 backup, run `docker compose up -d`. RTO ~30 min.
